refined_fr.refined_fr: CR-NGHE-003 Refined: The NGHE solution shall enable operators and tele-operators to achieve a measured energy efficiency of energy_per_ton ≤ E_target (customer-defined threshold) over the agreed evaluation period, through integrated monitoring, control optimization, and real-time feedback mechanisms.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is outcome-focused but lacks explicit connection to the mechanisms (monitoring, control, feedback) that enable efficiency achievement. Refinement clarifies that energy efficiency is not a passive property but an active capability requiring measurement, optimization logic, and operator awareness. This supports both direct operation and teleoperation modes.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure energy_per_ton = total_energy_used / total_ton_moved over the evaluation period. Verify that measured value ≤ E_target. Conduct trials across representative worksite conditions and operator skill levels (both on-site and remote).
sub_frs: CR-NGHE-003.1: The NGHE solution shall continuously measure and log energy consumption (kWh or equivalent) and work output (tons moved) with sufficient granularity to compute energy_per_ton.
CR-NGHE-003.2: The NGHE solution shall provide real-time energy efficiency feedback to operators (on-site and tele-operators) to enable informed control decisions.

CR-NGHE-003.3: The NGHE solution shall implement control strategies (e.g., load optimization, idle reduction, route planning) that minimize energy consumption while maintaining safety and productivity.
CR-NGHE-003.4: The NGHE solution shall maintain energy efficiency performance across varying worksite conditions, operator experience levels, and duty cycles.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Passive energy monitoring only (measure and log, no active optimization or feedback)
DP-B: Active energy management system with real-time feedback to operators and autonomous control optimization
DP-C: Hybrid approach: real-time feedback to operators with operator-directed control adjustments (no autonomous optimization)
DP-D: Centralized energy optimization via tele-operator commands with local telemetry collection
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-B: Active energy management system with real-time feedback to operators and autonomous control optimization

potential_sources: Energy measurement (telemetry) must couple with control actuation (Vehicle powertrain/hydraulics) to enable optimization—this is a necessary coupling, not a design flaw.
Feedback display (operator interface) couples with measurement system—acceptable, as both are part of the energy management ecosystem.
Autonomous optimization logic couples with safety constraints—mitigated by safety-critical validation and operator override capability.
coupling_check.notes: DP-B exhibits functional coupling (measurement → optimization → actuation → feedback loop) that is inherent to the problem, not a design defect. The coupling is manageable through clear interface contracts and safety validation.

assumptions_risks: Assumption: Vehicle powertrain and hydraulic systems can be instrumented with sufficient sensor fidelity to enable real-time energy measurement.
Assumption: Autonomous control optimization algorithms can be validated to safety-critical standards (high criticality per risk tailoring).
Risk: If sensor accuracy is poor, feedback and optimization will be unreliable, undermining the FR.
Risk: If optimization logic is not properly validated, it may conflict with safety constraints or operator intent.
Risk: Tele-operator latency may limit effectiveness of remote feedback-driven control adjustments.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: DP-B is sound but should be refined to explicitly include: (1) safety-critical validation of autonomous optimization, (2) operator override mechanisms, (3) sensor redundancy/validation for energy measurement, and (4) latency budgets for teleoperation feedback loops.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution is the top-level system boundary that integrates Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services modules. At this decomposition level, CR-NGHE-003 is assigned to NGHE Solution as the system-level owner, with sub-allocations to Vehicle (energy measurement and control optimization) and Teleoperation (operator feedback and remote command capability).

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Energy consumption data (kWh, power draw), work output metrics (tons moved, cycle time), and efficiency KPI (energy_per_ton) transmitted from Vehicle to Teleoperation and Safety Services for monitoring and feedback.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Operator commands (on-site or tele-operator) specifying work tasks, load targets, and operational modes; may include energy optimization directives.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Safety-critical alerts generated by Safety Services if energy optimization conflicts with safety constraints or if sensor faults are detected.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Communication channel between on-site Vehicle and remote Teleoperation module for telemetry uplink and command downlink; critical for tele-operator feedback and control.

module_containment_scores.notes: CR-NGHE-003 is a system-level KPI that spans multiple subsystems (Vehicle energy measurement, control optimization, Teleoperation feedback). NGHE Solution is the natural owner at this level, with clear sub-allocations to Vehicle and Teleoperation. Minimal diffusion risk if sub-allocations are explicit.

Vehicle owns energy measurement and control optimization hardware/logic, but cannot independently achieve the FR without Teleoperation feedback mechanisms and Safety Services validation. Assigning CR-NGHE-003 solely to Vehicle would create diffusion across Teleoperation and Safety Services.

Teleoperation provides operator feedback and remote command capability, but does not own energy measurement or vehicle control actuation. Assigning CR-NGHE-003 solely to Teleoperation would diffuse responsibility for the core energy optimization logic to Vehicle.

Safety Services validates that energy optimization does not violate safety constraints, but does not own measurement, optimization, or feedback mechanisms. Assigning CR-NGHE-003 to Safety Services would create high diffusion across Vehicle and Teleoperation.

assigned_im.ownership_candidate: NGHE Solution (system-level owner) with explicit sub-allocations to Vehicle (CR-NGHE-003.1, CR-NGHE-003.3) and Teleoperation (CR-NGHE-003.2), and coordination with Safety Services (CR-NGHE-003.4 safety validation).
assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: CR-NGHE-003 is best contained at the NGHE Solution level as a system-level KPI, with clear functional decomposition to sub-modules. This avoids diffusion and establishes a single point of accountability for energy efficiency performance while allowing Vehicle and Teleoperation to own their respective sub-functions.
containment_risks: Risk: If sub-allocations to Vehicle and Teleoperation are not explicitly documented, responsibility for energy efficiency may become ambiguous, leading to diffusion.

Risk: Integration testing across Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services is required to verify that the combined system achieves energy_per_ton ≤ E_target; failure to coordinate testing could mask integration issues.
Risk: Tele-operator latency or network unreliability could degrade the effectiveness of remote feedback-driven optimization, undermining the FR.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Assign CR-NGHE-003 to NGHE Solution with explicit sub-allocations: (1) Vehicle owns CR-NGHE-003.1 (measurement) and CR-NGHE-003.3 (control optimization), (2) Teleoperation owns CR-NGHE-003.2 (operator feedback), (3) Safety Services owns CR-NGHE-003.4 (safety validation). Document interface contracts (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, Network Link) to ensure integration clarity.

fr_dp_gate.reason: DP-B (active energy management with real-time feedback and autonomous optimization) directly addresses the refined FR by enabling continuous measurement, operator feedback, and control optimization. The DP is neither over-constrained nor under-specified; it provides a clear solution path to achieve energy_per_ton ≤ E_target. Functional coupling is inherent and manageable.

diffusion_risks: DP-B requires integration across Vehicle (measurement and control), Teleoperation (feedback), and Safety Services (validation). If module boundaries are not clearly defined, responsibility for energy optimization could diffuse.

Network Link reliability is critical for tele-operator feedback; latency or packet loss could degrade optimization effectiveness. This risk must be managed through interface contracts and performance budgets.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution as the system-level owner provides clear containment with sub-allocations to Vehicle and Teleoperation. Diffusion risk is low if sub-allocations are explicit and interface contracts are documented. Safety Services integration is necessary but does not create diffusion if its role (validation, not optimization) is clear.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification of CR-NGHE-003 is feasible through field trials measuring energy_per_ton over the evaluation period. Sub-allocations (CR-NGHE-003.1 through CR-NGHE-003.4) can be verified through unit tests (measurement accuracy, control logic), integration tests (feedback loop performance), and system tests (end-to-end energy efficiency across worksite conditions).
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Allocation is clear: NGHE Solution owns the system-level KPI, with sub-allocations to Vehicle (measurement and control), Teleoperation (feedback), and Safety Services (validation). Each sub-allocation maps to a specific sub-FR and can be traced to a module owner.

recommended_revisions: Document explicit sub-allocations of CR-NGHE-003 to Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services with clear ownership and interface contracts.
Define performance budgets for Network Link latency and reliability to ensure tele-operator feedback effectiveness.
Establish safety-critical validation procedures for autonomous energy optimization logic to mitigate high-criticality risk.
Define sensor redundancy and validation requirements for energy measurement to ensure measurement accuracy.

decision.justification: CR-NGHE-003 is not atomic and has been successfully decomposed into four sub-FRs (CR-NGHE-003.1 through CR-NGHE-003.4) that are more specific and actionable. The refined FR, selected DP, and assigned IM all pass gate review. The next level of decomposition is necessary to allocate sub-FRs to Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services modules and to define detailed design parameters for measurement, feedback, and optimization logic. Proceeding to the next level will clarify ownership boundaries and enable detailed design and verification planning.
next_level_sub_frs: CR-NGHE-003.1: The NGHE solution shall continuously measure and log energy consumption (kWh or equivalent) and work output (tons moved) with sufficient granularity to compute energy_per_ton.
CR-NGHE-003.2: The NGHE solution shall provide real-time energy efficiency feedback to operators (on-site and tele-operators) to enable informed control decisions.

CR-NGHE-003.3: The NGHE solution shall implement control strategies (e.g., load optimization, idle reduction, route planning) that minimize energy consumption while maintaining safety and productivity.
CR-NGHE-003.4: The NGHE solution shall maintain energy efficiency performance across varying worksite conditions, operator experience levels, and duty cycles.

assumptions: Vehicle powertrain and hydraulic systems can be instrumented with sufficient sensor fidelity for real-time energy measurement.
Autonomous control optimization algorithms can be validated to safety-critical standards.
Tele-operator latency is acceptable for feedback-driven control adjustments (latency budget to be defined).
Customer-defined E_target is achievable with DP-B (active energy management) under representative worksite conditions.
Operators (on-site and remote) will respond appropriately to energy efficiency feedback.
open_risks: Sensor accuracy and reliability for energy measurement; poor accuracy will undermine feedback and optimization.
Safety-critical validation of autonomous optimization logic; inadequate validation could create safety conflicts.
Network Link reliability and latency for tele-operator feedback; degradation could reduce optimization effectiveness.
Integration complexity across Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services; unclear interfaces could lead to diffusion.
Operator acceptance and adoption of energy efficiency feedback and optimization recommendations.
evidence_needed: Sensor specifications and accuracy data for energy measurement (power draw, consumption).
Preliminary design of autonomous energy optimization algorithms with safety analysis.
Network Link performance specifications (latency, reliability, bandwidth) for teleoperation.
Interface contracts (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, Network Link) with detailed message formats and timing requirements.
Field trial plan and acceptance criteria for energy_per_ton ≤ E_target verification.
Safety validation plan for energy optimization logic (FMEA, hazard analysis, etc.).
